title: Windows processes

headings.text: Key Attributes of a Windows Process:
Event ID 4688: A New Process Has Been Created
Event ID 4689: A Process Has Exited

overview: Windows processes are programs running in the background that carry out system operations and execute programs or applications on a Windows operating system. Each process has its own memory space and allocated resources. In the Windows environment, various activities such as account logins, file access, memory allocation, running DLLs, and application execut

study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: Key Attributes of a Windows Process:
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
